title: RequestCaseEmbedded
description: Published by IA3 (Matching) after computing a semantic embedding of the request. Dispatch Core uses this vector to find the professional with the highest cosine similarity via pgvector, for the semantic-matching strategies (S3/S4).

event_id.description: Unique ID for this specific event instance (not the request).

request_id.description: Correlation ID shared by every event belonging to the same patient request, from submission to closure. This is how Evaluation reconstructs a full request lifecycle across services.

version.description: Schema version for this event type, e.g. '1.0'. Bump on breaking changes so consumers can branch on it during migration.

produced_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the producing service emitted this event.

producer.description: Name of the service that published this event, e.g. 'ingestion', 'nlp-triage'. Useful for debugging and for Evaluation's traceability.

embedding.description: The embedding vector for this request's case. Length must match `dimension` and the pgvector column dimension in matching_db.

dimension.description: Length of the embedding vector, e.g. 384 for all-MiniLM-L6-v2.

embedding_model.description: Identifier of the sentence-transformers model used, e.g. 'sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2'. Needed so Evaluation/Dispatch never compare vectors produced by two different models.
